root_text.sn48.11:1.5: Idha, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako saddho hoti, saddahati tathāgatassa bodhiṁ:
root_text.sn48.11:1.6: ‘itipi so bhagavā arahaṁ sammāsambuddho vijjācaraṇasampanno sugato lokavidū anuttaro purisadammasārathi satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavā’ti—
root_text.sn48.11:2.2: Yaṁ kho, bhikkhave, cattāro sammappadhāne ārabbha vīriyaṁ paṭilabhati—
root_text.sn48.11:3.2: Yaṁ kho, bhikkhave, cattāro satipaṭṭhāne ārabbha satiṁ paṭilabhati—
root_text.sn48.11:4.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako vossaggārammaṇaṁ karitvā labhati samādhiṁ, labhati cittassa ekaggataṁ—
root_text.sn48.11:5.2: Idha, bhikkhave, ariyasāvako paññavā hoti udayatthagāminiyā paññāya samannāgato ariyāya nibbedhikāya sammā dukkhakkhayagāminiyā—

translation_text.sn48.11:1.3: The faculties of faith, energy, mindfulness, immersion, and wisdom.
translation_text.sn48.11:1.4: And what is the faculty of faith?
translation_text.sn48.11:1.5: It’s when a noble disciple has faith in the Realized One’s awakening:
translation_text.sn48.11:1.6: ‘That Blessed One is perfected, a fully awakened Buddha, accomplished in knowledge and conduct, holy, knower of the world, supreme guide for those fit for training, teacher of gods and humans, awakened, blessed.’
translation_text.sn48.11:1.7: This is called the faculty of faith.
translation_text.sn48.11:2.1: And what is the faculty of energy?
translation_text.sn48.11:2.2: The energy that’s gained in connection with the four right efforts.
translation_text.sn48.11:2.3: This is called the faculty of energy.
translation_text.sn48.11:3.1: And what is the faculty of mindfulness?
translation_text.sn48.11:3.2: The mindfulness that’s gained in connection with the four kinds of mindfulness meditation.
translation_text.sn48.11:3.3: This is called the faculty of mindfulness.
translation_text.sn48.11:4.1: And what is the faculty of immersion?
translation_text.sn48.11:4.2: It’s when a noble disciple, relying on letting go, gains immersion, gains unification of mind.
translation_text.sn48.11:4.3: This is called the faculty of immersion.
translation_text.sn48.11:5.1: And what is the faculty of wisdom?
translation_text.sn48.11:5.2: It’s when a noble disciple is wise. They have the wisdom of arising and passing away which is noble, penetrative, and leads to the complete ending of suffering.
translation_text.sn48.11:5.3: This is called the faculty of wisdom.
